response:
## Triage plan — top 10 findings

Ranked by `agent_risk` (with severity as tie-breaker), grouped by category, with action tag.

### IA / agent-context (3)
| # | id | charge | risk | action | why |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | crime_00002 | Missing Agent Context | 0.61 | **fix now** | Cheapest, highest-leverage. Adding `AGENTS.md` / `CLAUDE.md` unblocks every other agent edit in this repo. |
| 4 | crime_00009 | Concept Alias Drift (tenant/account/org/team/workspace) | 0.58 | **fix now** | Five names for one concept across docs + routes. Pick canonical term before extending any tenant logic. Document in the agent-context file from #1. |
| 7 | crime_00014 | Route Metadata Drift (`/settings/billing` labelled 4 ways) | 0.56 | **fix now** | Touches frontend + nav simultaneously — see #5 below; resolve together. |

### Frontend / duplication (2)
| # | id | charge | risk | action | why |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 3 | crime_00007 | String Sprinkles (`"enterprise"` in 4 files) | 0.60 | **fix now** | Plan-tier policy will drift across api/jobs/ui without a constant. Touch before any plan-related work. |
| 5 | crime_00012 | Duplicated Navigation Source (`/settings/billing` → "Plans" vs "Billing") | 0.57 | **fix now** | Bundle with #7 — one PR collapses nav source and aligns metadata. |

### Structural / testability (4)
| # | id | charge | risk | action | why |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 2 | crime_00005 | Temporal Recklessness (`src/date.ts`) | 0.61 | **fix now** | Date util module is the right place to inject a clock. Fixing here also unblocks #8. |
| 8 | crime_00004 | Temporal Recklessness (`src/billing.ts`, 7 sites) | 0.53 | **defer** | Same fix as #2, but spread across billing internals. Do it after #2 lands the clock abstraction so this is mechanical. |
| 9 | crime_00003 | Commented-Out Corpse (`billing.ts` L33–37) | 0.53 | **fix now** | One-line delete, zero risk; remove before next billing edit so agents don't revive `legacyRefund`. |
| 10 | crime_00008 | False Identity — `calculateRefundPreview` does I/O | 0.52 | **fix now** | Rename (or split pure calc from side effect) before another caller treats it as pure. Pairs naturally with #9 — same file. |

### Git-history (0)
No churn / bug-hotspot findings fired on this fixture — fixture has no meaningful git history. Nothing to triage in this category.

### Other / process (1)
| # | id | charge | risk | action | why |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 6 | crime_00013 | Unfinished Business (`src/todo.ts`, 424 markers/1k LOC) | 0.57 | **ignore** | Fixture-shaped: an entire file of markers is almost certainly an evals fixture, not load-bearing code. Suppress at the path level rather than triaging individually. If real: convert to issues, but don't block on it. |

### Recommended sequence
1. **PR 1 — agent context** (#1) — write `AGENTS.md` capturing repo commands + the canonical "tenant" term from #4.
2. **PR 2 — frontend/IA collapse** (#3, #5, #7) — one `plan-tier.ts` constant + one nav source of truth + route metadata aligned. Three findings, one diff.
3. **PR 3 — clock abstraction** (#2, then mechanical follow-up #8) — land `clock` in `src/date.ts`, then sweep `billing.ts`.
4. **PR 4 — billing hygiene** (#9, #10) — delete the corpse and rename `calculateRefundPreview` → `applyRefund` (or split). Same file, same review.
5. **Suppress** #6 in `crimes.config.json` rather than triaging.

Three deferred items in the original 22 (large file, logic-in-comments, the four low-severity orphaned routes / weak tests) can wait until the load-bearing work above is done — they're consequences of the structural issues, and several will dissolve once #2–#10 land.
